chunks.text: Research - EECS at Berkeley About Our Research Research is the foundation of Berkeley EECS Faculty and students work together on cutting-edge projects that cross disciplinary boundaries to improve everyday life and make a difference. Our leadership in research is the key to maintaining a vibrant curriculum that prepares our students to become leaders in their chosen fields. Explore EECS Research Areas Centers and Labs EECS researchers join together to explore a broad range of areas and ideas, from the tiniest nano particle to the latest Artificial Intelligence. Discover Our Research Groups Publications Technical Reports Ph.D. Dissertations Master’s Theses and Reports Patents PVDF cockroach-inspired robot (photo: Stephen McNally) Areas Our Researchers delve deeply into a wide variety of subjects, from the Chisel language for advanced hardware design to wearable sensor arrays for personalized medicine. Look Into Our Research Areas EECS Joint Colloquium The weekly EECS Colloquium offers our faculty a chance to invite speakers offering a wide range of experiences covering topics in both Electrical Engineering and Computer Science to engage with the department. View Lectures Undergraduate Research Research allows you to pursue your interests, to learn something new, to hone your problem-solving skills and to challenge yourself in new ways. Working on a faculty-initiated research project gives you the opportunity work closely with a mentor–a faculty member or other experienced researcher. With a self-initiated research project, you leave Berkeley with a product that represents the distillation of your interests and studies and, possibly, a real contribution to knowledge. Read
